aws rate limit (per second) for instance creation. This is used to avoid hitting the request limits from aws, which can vary based on the region, and the size of the cluster being created. Try lowering this limit when hitting 'Request limit exceeded' errors. (default 2)

Only attach the boot disk. No additional volumes will be provisioned even if specified.
Disk caching behavior for attached storage. Valid values are: none, read-only, read-write. Not applicable to Ultra disks. (default "none")

The name of the VNet to use (default "common")
Size in GB of network disk volume, only used if local-ssd=false (default 500)

Zones for cluster, where a zone is a location and availability zone separated by a dash. If zones are formatted as Location-AZ:N where N is an integer, the zone will be repeated N times. The cloud provider(s) to use when creating new vm instances: [aws gce azure ibm local] (default [gce])
The underlying file system(ext4/zfs/xfs/f2fs/btrfs). ext4 is used by default. (default "ext4")

The label(s) to be used when creating new vm instances, must be in '--label name=value' format and value can't be empty string after trimming space, a value that has space must be quoted by single quotes, gce label name only allows hyphens (-), underscores (_), lowercase characters, numbers and international characters (default [usage=roachprod])
Lifetime of the cluster (default 12h0m0s)
Use local SSD (default true)
Mount the local SSD with the "-o nobarrier" flag. Ignored if --local-ssd=false is specified. (default true)
Total number of nodes, distributed across all clouds (default 4)
OS disk volume size in GB (default 10)
Username to run under, detect if blank (default "will.choe")
